# SL-CANON-016: google-selected-canonical-differs

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

Google selected a canonical different from the user-declared canonical.

## Metadata

- Category: Canonical and hreflang (canonical-hreflang)
- Default severity: warning
- Confidence: certain
- Scope: page
- Provider scope: google
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- google

## Required Evidence

- declared-canonical
- google-canonical
- observed-at
- fetched-at

## When This Triggers

Compare declared canonical with latest Google URL Inspection selected canonical.

## False Positives

False positives are usually caused by stale or sampled external observations.
Check the observation date, freshness, and provider payload before suppressing.

## How To Fix

Review canonical signals and align declared canonical, internal links, sitemap,
and content.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/canonical/google-canonical-differs

Fixed direction:

- Review canonical signals and align declared canonical, internal links,
  sitemap, and content.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.

## Implementation Surfaces

- Google integration must provide dated, freshness-aware observations before
  this rule can use Google evidence.
